steps.title: Identify the cause-and-effect relationship
steps.content: The first independent clause describes what Elodea canadensis does—it absorbs cadmium and stores it safely. The second independent clause states what happens as a result—cadmium concentrations in the soil decrease. The transition must show that the plant's action produces this outcome.

steps.title: Match the causal signal to the choices
steps.content:
“Accordingly” means as a result or therefore, and it directly connects the plant's cadmium absorption to the soil's declining cadmium levels.

The other three—”specifically” (which introduces a specific example), “nevertheless” (which introduces contrast), and “in addition” (which adds information at the same level)—fail to express this consequence.

D is correct.
